title: The 16MB BSON document size limit
description: A single document cannot exceed 16MB, which caps how much can be embedded before a redesign is forced.
body: Reference for embedding decisions. Every BSON document has a hard 16MB ceiling, so an unbounded embedded array or accumulated history eventually fails to update. Use when deciding whether growth-prone data can be embedded. Do not embed a collection that grows without limit; updates start to fail with a document-too-large error as it nears the cap. Validation signals include the failing update reporting BSONObjectTooLarge and Object.bsonsize() showing the document near 16MB. Risk boundary: when content is legitimately large (files, blobs), use GridFS or external object storage instead of embedding, and escalate any model that assumes unlimited document growth.
